 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ki42","title":"E2E Tests: Swap Meet (Community Marketplace)","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-75p.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the Swap Meet (community marketplace) with detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

### Browse & Discovery Tests
1. **Swap Meet Home Page**
   - Featured prompts section displays correctly
   - "Prompt of the day" is highlighted
   - Category filter chips work
   - Pagination loads more prompts
   - Empty state shows for new users

2. **Browse by Category**
   - Category pages show correct prompts
   - Prompt counts match filter results
   - Multiple category selection works
   - Clear filters resets view

3. **Sorting**
   - Sort by "Newest" shows recent first
   - Sort by "Most Popular" uses save count
   - Sort by "Highest Rated" uses rating
   - Sort by "Trending" uses recent activity
   - Sort persists across navigation

### Search Tests
4. **Search Functionality**
   - Search input has autocomplete
   - Search results are relevant
   - Highlights matched terms
   - "No results" state displays
   - Search within category works
   - Search history/suggestions work

### Prompt Detail Tests
5. **Community Prompt View**
   - Full prompt content displays
   - Author info with avatar and reputation
   - Usage stats (saves, copies, rating)
   - Related prompts section
   - Copy to clipboard works
   - Save to library works

6. **Prompt Interactions**
   - Rating prompt (thumbs up/down or stars)
   - Cannot rate own prompt
   - Can change rating
   - Report prompt for moderation
   - Share prompt link works

### Publishing Tests
7. **Publish Prompt to Swap Meet**
   - "Publish" button on private prompt
   - Publish modal shows guidelines
   - Must agree to community terms
   - Published prompt appears in Swap Meet
   - Author receives notification of saves/ratings

8. **Unpublish Prompt**
   - "Unpublish" button on public prompt
   - Confirmation modal warns about removal
   - Unpublished prompt removed from Swap Meet
   - Existing saves remain in users' libraries

### Quality & Moderation Tests
9. **Content Quality**
   - Low-quality prompts are flagged
   - Reported prompts go to moderation queue
   - Removed prompts show "Removed" state
   - Author notified of moderation actions

## Logging Requirements
- Log search queries and result counts
- Log filter/sort selections
- Log user interactions (saves, ratings, reports)
- Log publication events
- Log moderation actions
- Screenshot key states and error conditions

## Test Infrastructure
- Seed community with sample prompts
- Create multiple test users for interactions
- Test with various content types
- Clean up test content after suite

## Files to Create
- `e2e/swapmeet/browse.spec.ts`
- `e2e/swapmeet/search.spec.ts`
- `e2e/swapmeet/prompt-detail.spec.ts`
- `e2e/swapmeet/publish.spec.ts`
- `e2e/swapmeet/interactions.spec.ts`
- `e2e/lib/swapmeet-helpers.ts`

## Success Criteria
- All Swap Meet flows have E2E coverage
- Search returns relevant results
- Publishing/unpublishing works correctly
- Interactions (save, rate, report) work
- Performance: < 3s page load times

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","title":"Build admin dashboard and user management","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-fxm. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Build admin dashboard for platform management.

## Access Control
- Add is_admin boolean to user_profiles
- Middleware/layout check for /admin/* routes
- Admin-only API endpoints return 403 for non-admins

## Pages

### /admin (Dashboard Overview)
Stats cards:
- Total users (+ new this week)
- Active subscribers (MRR)
- Total prompts/packs/skills
- Pending moderation count

Charts:
- User signups over time
- Subscription revenue over time

Recent activity:
- New signups
- New content
- Reports

### /admin/users
User management table.

Columns:
- Email, name, tier, status, joined
- Actions: view, impersonate, suspend

Features:
- Search by email/name
- Filter by tier, status
- Sort columns
- Click to view user detail

### /admin/users/:id
User detail page.

Shows:
- Profile info
- Subscription status
- Content created
- Reports received
- Admin actions log

Actions:
- Adjust subscription
- Suspend/unsuspend
- Delete account

### /admin/moderation
Content reports queue.

List of reports:
- Reporter, content, reason, date
- Quick actions: dismiss, warn, remove
- Click to review in detail

## API Endpoints
- GET /api/admin/stats
- GET /api/admin/users
- GET /api/admin/users/:id
- PUT /api/admin/users/:id (update status)
- GET /api/admin/reports
- PUT /api/admin/reports/:id (action)

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-klux","title":"EPIC: Public Profiles & Reputation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-61i.

## Overview
Public user profiles and author reputation system for the community.

## Public Profile Page (/user/[username])

### Profile Header
- Avatar
- Display name
- Username
- Bio
- Join date
- Social links
- Reputation badges

### Content Tabs
- Public Prompts
- Public Packs
- Public Skills
- Activity (recent public actions)

### Stats Display
- Total public prompts
- Total saves received
- Average rating
- Member since

## Reputation System

### Reputation Score
Calculated from:
- Number of public prompts (+10 each)
- Total saves received (+1 each)
- Positive ratings received (+2 each)
- Age of account
- Featured content (+50 each)

### Badges
- "New Member" - < 30 days
- "Contributor" - 5+ public prompts
- "Popular" - 100+ total saves
- "Top Rated" - Average rating > 90%
- "Featured Author" - Has featured content
- "Founding Member" - Early adopter

### Display
- Badge icons on profile
- Reputation score (optional to show)
- Badge tooltips with criteria

## Database
- Add to user_profiles: username (unique), bio, social_links, is_public
- user_badges (user_id, badge_type, awarded_at)
- Calculate reputation score via materialized view or on-demand

## Privacy
- Users can make profile private (content still public if visibility=public)
- Can hide reputation score
- Can choose which badges to display

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l1t7","title":"Typography Standardization: Eliminate arbitrary text sizes","description":"# Epic: Typography Standardization

## Background & Problem Statement

The UI overhaul established a 5-size typography scale in globals.css:
- text-xs (12px)
- text-sm (14px)
- text-base (16px)
- text-lg (18px)
- text-xl+ (headings)

However, several components still use arbitrary values like \`text-[11px]\` and \`text-[10px]\`, which:
1. Break the design system's rhythm
2. Are harder to maintain
3. Create subtle inconsistencies between similar elements

## Files Affected
1. PromptDetailModal.tsx - 3 instances of text-[11px]
2. SpotlightSearch.tsx - 1 instance of text-[11px], 3 instances of text-[10px]

## The Fix
Replace all arbitrary text sizes with the nearest standard size:
- \`text-[11px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +1px - negligible visual difference)
- \`text-[10px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +2px - slightly larger but more readable)

## Implementation Note
**Typography fixes are included in the color fix tasks:**
- g0oe (PromptDetailModal) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section
- usn5 (SpotlightSearch) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section

This keeps related changes together (same lines of code) and reduces PR churn.

## Design Rationale
- 10-11px text is often too small for comfortable reading
- The extra 1-2px improves accessibility without breaking layouts
- Consistency trumps pixel-perfect sizing for this use case

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero instances of \`text-[Npx]\` in component files
- [ ] All text uses standard Tailwind classes
- [ ] No layout shifts from size changes
- [ ] Text remains readable at all sizes

## Validation
The color validation tests (tzmc) include a typography consistency check that will verify no arbitrary text sizes remain.

## Dependencies
- Typography fixes are bundled into g0oe and usn5 tasks
- tzmc validates the result

## Estimated Scope
- 2 files (via g0oe and usn5)
- 7 class replacements
- Minimal testing required","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:37:31.250583614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T22:54:54.515685149Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T22:54:54.515685149Z","close_reason":"Fixed all text-[Npx] patterns: Nav.tsx and BottomTabBar.tsx now use text-xs. Zero arbitrary text sizes remain in component files.","compaction_level":0}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-zosz","title":"Implement referral program for user growth","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-x85c.

## Task
Implement a referral program to encourage users to invite others.

## Why This Matters
- Viral growth loop (users bring users)
- Lower customer acquisition cost
- Higher quality leads (trusted referral)
- Rewards loyal users

## Program Structure

### Referral Rewards
**Referrer (existing user):**
- 1 month free Premium for each successful referral
- OR credit toward subscription
- Cap at 12 months free per year

**Referee (new user):**
- Extended trial (30 days instead of 14)
- OR 20% off first month

### Referral Flow
1. User gets unique referral link/code
2. Shares with friend
3. Friend signs up using link
4. Friend converts to paid
5. Both get rewards

### Referral Link
- Format: jeffreysprompts.com/r/[CODE]
- Unique per user
- Trackable

### Success Criteria for Referral
- Referee must convert to paid subscription
- Minimum 1 month paid to count
- No self-referral

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const referralCodes = pgTable("referral_codes", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  code: text("code").notNull().unique(),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});

export const referrals = pgTable("referrals", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  referrerId: uuid("referrer_id").references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  refereeId: uuid("referee_id").references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  codeUsed: text("code_used").notNull(),
  status: text("status").default("pending"), // pending, converted, rewarded
  referrerReward: text("referrer_reward"),
  refereeReward: text("referee_reward"),
  convertedAt: timestamp("converted_at"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

## API Endpoints
- GET /api/referral/code - Get user's referral code
- POST /api/referral/apply - Apply referral code on signup
- GET /api/referral/stats - Get referral statistics

## UI Components
- ReferralCard - Shows code and share buttons
- ReferralStats - Shows successful referrals
- ShareModal - Easy sharing to social/email

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/referrals.ts
- src/app/api/referral/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/referrals/page.tsx
- src/components/referral/referral-card.tsx
- src/components/referral/share-modal.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Referral tracking works
- Rewards applied correctly
- Easy to share
- Fraud prevention

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-zuqk","title":"Implement staff picks and featured content","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0eiu.

## Task
Allow admins to curate featured content for discovery.

## Why This Matters
- Highlight quality content
- Editorial control
- Guide new users
- Reward creators

## Features

### 1. Staff Picks Selection
- Admin can mark as staff pick
- Optional expiration date
- Order/priority control
- Category-specific picks

### 2. Featured Sections
- Homepage featured carousel
- Category page features
- Search results boost
- Email newsletter picks

### 3. Badges
- 'Staff Pick' badge on content
- 'Featured' badge
- Time-limited visibility

## Database
```typescript
export const featuredContent = pgTable('featured_content', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // What is featured
  resourceType: text('resource_type').notNull(), // prompt, collection, pack, profile
  resourceId: uuid('resource_id').notNull(),
  
  // Feature details
  featureType: text('feature_type').notNull(), // staff_pick, featured, spotlight
  category: text('category'), // Optional category (for category-specific features)
  
  // Display
  position: integer('position').default(0),
  headline: text('headline'), // Optional override headline
  
  // Timing
  startAt: timestamp('start_at').defaultNow(),
  endAt: timestamp('end_at'),
  
  // Tracking
  featuredBy: uuid('featured_by').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  index('idx_featured_type').on(table.featureType),
  index('idx_featured_active').on(table.startAt, table.endAt),
]);
```

## Admin UI
- Content browser with 'Feature' action
- Featured content manager
- Drag-drop reordering
- Schedule features

## API
```typescript
// GET /api/featured?type=staff_pick&limit=10
// POST /api/admin/featured (add)
// DELETE /api/admin/featured/[id] (remove)
// PATCH /api/admin/featured/[id] (update order)
```

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/featured.ts
- src/app/api/featured/route.ts
- src/app/api/admin/featured/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/admin/featured/page.tsx
- src/components/featured/staff-pick-badge.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Admins can feature content
- Featured content displays prominently
- Badges visible on content
